extraction.one_line_summary: DEMAR Arnaud won stage 18 sprint after finishing last two consecutive days in Pyrenees, delivered by flawless Groupama-FDJ leadout.
tactical_patterns: Demar recovered from back-to-back lanterne rouge finishes in mountains to sprint victory, revealing pure sprinters' recovery superiority over all-rounders.
Groupama-FDJ controlled bunch sprint from start, closing gaps potentially too early but executing flawless final kilometer positioning.
ALAPHILIPPE abandoned peloton 7km from finish to rest for stage 19 breakaway, prioritizing polka dot security over meaningless sprint placement.
Third-week sprint featured subdued peloton pace allowing puncheurs like FINNEY to contest top-10 against fatigued pure sprinters.

rider_signals.DEMAR Arnaud: positive: surviving mountain brutality then sprinting optimally reveals exceptional recovery capacity for pure sprinters in third week
rider_signals.FINNEY Taylor: positive: 9th place mixing with specialists shows third-week sprint degradation creates opportunities for versatile domestiques
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: visibly exhausted post-sprint and surfing wheels suggests green jersey defense costing him usual finishing power
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: tactical abandonment 7km out to conserve energy for stage 19 attack shows polka dot math already secured
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: described as one-off poor mountain performance requiring reassurance about stage 19 form from commentators
rider_signals.GUARNIERI Jacopo: positive: leadout man execution described as beautiful, key to Demar's positioning entering final corner
